behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because failure appears at module handoff (diff extraction output passed into review-input builder), not inside one pure function. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because this can be reproduced with local diff fixtures; behavior text does not require a real PR/API. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is only about scoping input lines, not real network or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire diff-parser + input-construction modules with fixture PR patches containing unchanged sensitive lines; assert produced review payload includes only changed hunks and excludes unchanged file content.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because mapping logic crosses diff parser + comment payload builder. Winner vs System -> System wins because true failure boundary is GitHub diff-position semantics on a real PR; mocks can accept invalid coordinates that GitHub rejects/misplaces. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-output-quality requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed positions and verify via GitHub API/UI metadata that each comment lands on the intended changed line in the diff.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because prompt assembly + result parsing are involved. Winner vs System -> System would cover API plumbing, but Agentic is required because correctness depends on real model judgment ('identifies at least one real defect'). Rejected Workflow: behavior asks for one detection outcome, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the analyzer with a real model on a seeded buggy PR diff (known bug ground truth); pass if at least one returned finding correctly matches the introduced defect category/location.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because this is cross-module state consistency (inline-post response IDs handed to summary composer). Integration vs System -> Integration wins: local in-process stubs for post responses are sufficient; behavior text does not require real PR/API semantics. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute run orchestration with deterministic finding fixtures and stubbed inline-post responses; assert each summary entry references an inline comment ID emitted in the same run context and none from prior runs.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because drops typically occur during orchestration/serialization boundaries between finding collector and summary payload builder. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because the drop can be detected before network I/O with local wiring. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior is completeness of transfer, not model quality or multi-agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Feed multiple inline findings (including edge cases like duplicate file paths and multi-line comments) through collector -> summary-builder pipeline; assert payload cardinality and IDs exactly match all inline findings.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because this is end-to-end module wiring. Winner vs System -> System wins due explicit 'real pull request' and real API side effects (read diff, post comments, post summary). Rejected Agentic: behavior text says 'fixed precomputed finding set', disqualifying dependence on model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline against a real sandbox PR with static findings; assert diff retrieval succeeds, all inline comments are created, and one top-level summary is posted with expected references.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because multi-module orchestration is involved. Winner vs System -> System is necessary for real PR I/O, but Agentic is minimum to catch failures tied to model-produced findings ('model-generated findings'). Rejected Workflow: behavior describes one agentic decision stage (analyze) followed by deterministic posting, not multiple agentic stages with sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full automation on a real test PR using real model inference; assert run completes autonomously, produces non-empty valid findings, posts inline comments successfully, and posts summary consistent with posted comments.
